title: Design System: NYC Food Cart Location Analysis

headline.purpose: The dashboard title, once per page.

label.purpose: Control labels and card stats; bold labels, regular values.

components.name: Location Card
components.description: Ranked station card in the sidebar list: numbered title plus bold-label stats.
components.html:
1. W 21 St & 6 Ave

Score: 0.982

Total Trips: 48,112

Lunch Trips: 4,301
components.css: .ds-location-card { padding: 12px; background: #f9fafb; border-radius: 8px; border: 1px solid #e5e7eb; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #1f2937; } .ds-location-card h3 { margin: 0 0 6px 0; font-size: 15px; } .ds-location-card p { margin: 4px 0; font-size: 13px; }

components.name: Control Box
components.description: A labeled full-width select — the sidebar's standard filter instrument.
components.css: .ds-control-box { margin: 18px 0; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #1f2937; } .ds-control-box label { display: block; font-weight: 700; margin-bottom: 8px; font-size: 14px; } .ds-control-box select { width: 100%; font-size: 14px; padding: 4px; } .ds-control-box select:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #4b5563; outline-offset: 1px; }

components.name: Range Control
components.description: Threshold slider with a live value readout (minimum lunch-hour trips).
components.html: 1200
components.css: .ds-range-box { margin: 18px 0; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #1f2937; } .ds-range-box label { display: block; font-weight: 700; margin-bottom: 8px; font-size: 14px; } .ds-range-box input[type=range] { width: 100%; } .ds-range-box span { font-size: 13px; color: #4b5563; }

components.name: Legend Panel
components.description: Score-color legend: the only chrome element allowed to show data colors.
components.html:
Score Color

High score

Medium score

Low score
components.css: .ds-legend { margin: 24px 0; padding: 14px; background: #f9fafb; border-radius: 8px; border: 1px solid #e5e7eb; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #1f2937; } .ds-legend h3 { margin-top: 0; font-size: 15px; } .ds-legend p { font-size: 14px; margin: 6px 0; } .ds-dot { display: inline-block; width: 12px; height: 12px; border-radius: 50%; margin-right: 8px; } .ds-high { background: #16a34a; } .ds-medium { background: #f59e0b; } .ds-low { background: #dc2626; }

components.name: Map Marker Popup
components.description: Leaflet popup content: bold station name over a compact stat list.
components.html:
W 21 St & 6 Ave
Food Cart Score: 0.982
Total Trips: 48112
Lunch-Hour Trips: 4301
components.css: .ds-popup { font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 13px; color: #1f2937; line-height: 1.5; }

narrative.overview: This is a working tool for spatial decisions. The map is the hero: it owns the majority of the viewport, and every other element exists to sharpen what the map shows. The sidebar is a bank of precise instruments rendered in quiet neutrals so the scored markers carry all the color. The personality is clean, analytical, trustworthy: the interface of an analyst who trusts their data and doesn't decorate it. The system explicitly rejects the generic Bootstrap dashboard: no default-widget clutter, no admin-panel chrome, no panels stacked for their own sake.
keyCharacteristics: Map-first layout: a fixed 380px instrument sidebar, everything else is map.
Neutral gray-blue chrome; saturated color is reserved for data (score markers).
Flat surfaces separated by hairline borders and background tints, never shadows.
Refined and restrained components: minimal styling, function-first.

rules.name: The Data-Owns-Color Rule
rules.body: Saturated color (green, amber, red) appears only on data: markers, legend dots, and score indicators. Chrome, controls, and text stay neutral. If a button or panel is colorful, it's wrong.

rules.name: The Two-Channel Rule
rules.body: Score encoding may not rely on hue alone — the green/amber/red trio is a known colorblind hazard. Pair color with rank position, a label, or shape whenever scores are shown.

rules.name: The One-Face Rule
rules.body: One font family, everywhere. Hierarchy is weight and size only.

rules.name: The No-Shadow Rule
rules.body: box-shadow is prohibited. Layering is tint plus hairline, nothing else.

dos: Do keep the map dominant — the sidebar stays at 380px and never grows extra panels.
Do reserve #16a34a / #f59e0b / #dc2626 for data encoding only.
Do separate surfaces with 1px #e5e7eb hairlines and background tints (#f4f6f8 → #ffffff → #f9fafb).
Do format scores to three decimals and keep stat labels bold, values regular.
Do pair score color with rank, label, or shape (The Two-Channel Rule).
donts: Don't build a "generic Bootstrap dashboard" — no default-widget clutter, no admin-panel chrome.
Don't use box-shadow anywhere (The No-Shadow Rule).
Don't put saturated color on chrome, buttons, or headers.
Don't add controls that don't change what the map or list shows.
Don't introduce a second font family.
